extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the Rome sprint after following VIVIANI's wheel perfectly, capitalizing on Quick-Step's positioning chaos in final 150 meters.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step deployed three leadout men for VIVIANI but spacing errors allowed BENNETT to infiltrate wheel position at 600m mark.
BENNETT deliberately sat two-off until final 150m instead of traditional leadout sprint, exploiting his superior top-end speed burst.
FROOME's GC win required reactive rather than front-running tactics throughout, suggesting Sky adapted strategy when leader lacks peak form.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: consistently arrived too late from too far back on other sprint stages, showing positioning skills developed during race
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: won points jersey but leadout execution failures in Rome finale exposed vulnerability when Quick-Step spacing collapses
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: first British Giro winner rode defensively from behind rather than typical Sky domination, indicating tactical flexibility when underprepared
